note: strings[] is ordered low→high. Focus is ALTERNATE tunings (E standard not catalogued). songs[].t references tunings[].id; expand to strings[] on load.

notes.songsterr: Imported from the Songsterr catalogue; reflects the transcriber's tuning, not verified against the recording.
notes.conflict: A second transcription uses this tuning. Unresolved — check by ear.

tunings.name: E Standard
tunings.desc: Standard tuning. Most songs live here.

tunings.name: E♭ Standard
tunings.desc: Half step down. Hendrix, Guns N' Roses, lots of metal.

tunings.name: D Standard
tunings.desc: Whole step down. Thicker, heavier body.

tunings.name: C# Standard
tunings.desc: Minor third down. Dark and low.

tunings.name: C Standard
tunings.desc: Two whole steps down. Heavy low end.

tunings.name: Drop D
tunings.desc: Low E dropped to D. One-finger power chords.

tunings.name: Drop C
tunings.desc: D standard with the 6th dropped to C.

tunings.name: Drop B
tunings.desc: Very low and heavy. Slipknot Vol. 3 territory.

tunings.name: DADGAD
tunings.desc: Modal, droning, open. Celtic and Zeppelin.

tunings.name: Open D
tunings.desc: Strums an open D major. Slide favourite.

tunings.name: Open E
tunings.desc: Open D shapes at standard pitch. Bottleneck blues.

tunings.name: Open G
tunings.desc: Keith Richards territory (he drops the 6th string entirely).

tunings.name: Drop C#
tunings.desc: E♭ standard with the 6th string dropped to C#.

tunings.name: Drop A
tunings.desc: Lower than Drop B. Slipknot go here for a handful of songs.

tunings.name: E1 B1 E2 A2 D3 G3 B3 E4
A#1 G2 C3 F3 A3 D4
C2 A2 D3 G3 B3 E4
F#2 B2 E3 A3 D4 G4
D1 D2 G2 C3 F3 A3 D4
C2 G2 C3 D#3 G3 C4
A1 D2 G2 C3 F3 A3
B1 F#2 B2 F3 G#3 C#4
B1 F#2 B2 E4 G#3 C#4

songs.note: Vol. 3: (The Subliminal Verses) was tracked in Drop B. Many tabs are written in E standard for convenience — the record is not.
Open E with a capo at fret 3 (sounds as open G). Played with a slide/bottleneck.
Original recording is a whole step down, D standard. No capo.
Page's signature DADGAD drone. The riff relies on the open D/A strings ringing.
Drop D. The main riff is built on the dropped 6th string.
Open G on FIVE strings: Keith removes the low 6th entirely. On a normal six-string, tune to open G and simply ignore the 6th string. Intentionally not identical to the six-string open-g entry.
Standard tuning — no retune needed.
Hendrix almost always tuned a half step down.
Drop C. Verify by ear against the record — SOAD used several low tunings across albums.
Drop B. Same Vol. 3 sessions as Vermilion Pt. 2.
Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.
Drop A, not Drop B. Slipknot drop below their usual Drop B for this one. Transposed Drop B arrangements of it exist, but the record is Drop A.
Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.
Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.
Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not.
D standard. Written and demoed in E standard, then dropped a whole step on Bob Rock's suggestion.

songs.song: The Thing That Should Not Be
songs.note: Sources disagree: many transcriptions say D standard, older ones say Drop D, and Kirk Hammett has been quoted saying C#. Check by ear against the record.
D standard.
D standard.
D standard.
E♭ standard — the odd one out on an album that is mostly E standard.
Load is E♭ standard throughout except this track, which drops the 6th string to C#.
Playable either way; several tabbers note the band plays it in Drop D.
Drop C — the heavier low tuning of Rammstein's later records.
Drop C — the heavier low tuning of Rammstein's later records.
